result:
**Goal: Add a Traffic Flow mode** — a Nagel-Schreckenberg cellular automaton simulating multi-lane highway traffic where vehicles accelerate, brake, and lane-change, producing phantom traffic jams, stop-and-go waves, flow-density phase transitions, and on-ramp bottleneck dynamics, with a real-time fundamental diagram (flow vs. density) overlay.

This fills a notable gap — the project has 130+ modes spanning physics, biology, fractals, and game theory, but no transportation/traffic dynamics. The Nagel-Schreckenberg model is a foundational CA application (alongside Game of Life), is visually intuitive in a terminal (cars as colored chars moving on ASCII roads), and exhibits rich emergent behavior: a single random brake event can cascade into a standing wave that persists long after the cause is gone. Presets would include open highway, bottleneck, roundabout, and grid intersection scenarios.
